topic: World History Topics (two, same as SL)
subject: history

items.q: Why did Lenin introduce the NEP in 1921?
items.correct: War Communism had collapsed the economy and provoked the Kronstadt revolt; the NEP allowed limited private trade to restore production and stabilise Bolshevik rule.
items.misconception: The NEP was Lenin's plan to permanently switch Russia to capitalism.
items.random_wrong: The NEP was introduced by Stalin to fund the Five-Year Plans.
items.why: Lenin called the NEP a strategic retreat, not a destination. Students miss the tension between socialist ideology and pragmatic recovery — central to understanding 1920s USSR.

items.q: Why did Japan attack Pearl Harbor?
items.correct: To cripple the US Pacific Fleet and buy time to secure resource-rich Southeast Asia after the US oil embargo threatened Japanese expansion.
items.misconception: Japan attacked because it wanted to conquer America.
items.random_wrong: Japan attacked to support Germany's invasion of the USSR.
items.why: Japan never planned to conquer the US — the goal was a defensive perimeter. Students assume the attack signalled invasion ambitions when it was a desperate strategic gamble.

items.q: Why did Huguenots migrate to England from the 1680s?
items.correct: Louis XIV's 1685 revocation of the Edict of Nantes ended toleration of French Protestants; many fled persecution to Protestant England, bringing skills like silk weaving.
items.misconception: Huguenots came to England because France was poorer than England.
items.random_wrong: Huguenots were Catholic refugees fleeing Cromwell.
items.why: Huguenots were Protestants fleeing Catholic persecution, not economic migrants. Students project modern migration motives onto religious refugees.

items.q: Which 1938 conference ceded the Sudetenland to Germany?
items.why: Correct answer: Munich. “Locarno” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Who signed the 1939 non-aggression pact for the USSR?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Molotov. “Litvinov” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what month and year did Hitler remilitarise the Rhineland?
items.why: Exact answer: 1936 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
